subtitle: Senior Innovation & Applications Leader -- AI/GenAI Tool Evaluation | Application Governance | Knowledge-Worker Change
summary_text: Technology executive with a structured, repeatable methodology for evaluating, selecting, and governing enterprise AI and automation tools -- now bringing that discipline to legal-industry innovation. Currently consulting on AI architecture, automation, and implementation; built a scoring rubric, evaluation flow, and capability taxonomy adopted by a national CX consulting firm to compare enterprise AI platforms on governance, integration fit, vendor lock-in, and failure modes at scale. Earlier: 14 years as the first CTO at Money Management International (a national 501(c)(3) financial services nonprofit) governing the full application lifecycle of a homegrown ERP/CRM platform across 12+ executive stakeholders, vendor selection, and platform evolution -- under continuous regulatory scrutiny (HIPAA, PCI-DSS, CARD Act, CFPB across all 50 states). 17 years before that as founder of DMLCo, delivering custom digital solutions to professional services clients.

bullets: Designed an algorithmic AI architecture evaluation framework -- scoring rubric, capability taxonomy, evaluation flow -- adopted by a national CX consulting firm to compare enterprise AI platforms with explicit governance, integration-fit, and capability dimensions; the discipline transfers cleanly to legal-tech tool evaluation (Relativity vs. Reveal vs. emerging GenAI).

Provide client-facing advisory on generative and agentic AI architectures, automation, and implementation; translate executive priorities into architectural trade-offs and concrete platform decisions.
Hands-on AI-assisted engineering daily with Claude and GitHub Copilot; published thinking on AI adoption realities, the automation-vs-AI distinction, and reflexive AI governance practice on LinkedIn.

Directed enterprise technology for a national 501(c)(3) nonprofit -- under continuous regulatory scrutiny across all 50 states plus federal regulators including the CFPB, with HIPAA, PCI-DSS, and CARD Act overlays.

Governed the full application lifecycle of MMI's homegrown ERP/CRM platform for 8 years -- vendor selection, requirements methodology, stakeholder prioritization across 12+ executive and operations stakeholders, and platform evolution from single-product tool to variable multi-product platform across a 45-50 person delivery organization.

Operated multi-factor investment prioritization model (ROI x user satisfaction x efficiency x cross-departmental equity) governing sprint-over-sprint decisions; the equity mechanism prevented any single function from dominating or being ignored.

Built Enterprise PMO from scratch; consolidated cross-functional project governance across IT and non-IT functions; delivered 25% improvement in project budget and time adherence across a $30-50M annual portfolio.
Sustained 100% PCI-DSS certification across multiple audit cycles alongside HIPAA and CARD Act compliance; managed $15M+ IT operating budget within $30-50M total IT spend.
Led full enterprise contact center AI transformation -- intelligent routing, AI-assisted coaching and monitoring, integrated knowledge bots -- with zero service disruption on day-one go-live.

Drove technology adoption across credit counselors, compliance officers, and state-regulated operations -- highly educated, skeptical knowledge workers, sustained over 14 years; built and led a 35+ direct (75+ extended) technology team with highest retention and satisfaction in the enterprise.
Founded and ran a web development and IT consulting firm for 17 years -- full P&L ownership; delivered custom digital solutions for dozens of clients including professional services firms.
Architected and operated a proprietary commercial-grade CMS supporting dozens of production sites; direct client engagement across discovery, scope, architecture, build, and ongoing support.

projects.title: github.com/mknnmknn/about-mknn
projects.desc: Overview of public projects, with access to private repositories on request. Featured project: career-ops-dml -- a private fork of santifer/career-ops, customized into the LLM-assisted pipeline used daily in my job search. Working code extended alongside Claude Code (Node.js, Claude API, Playwright, Go).
